_note: The orientations you can consult before a reading — the 'pause and listen' device made into data. Each voice has a glyph (no copyrighted portraits — abstract marks only), a one-line tagline, and 'how' (how to read in this voice). Every voice's `link` opens the Intention Setting course at its own subsection (?course=intention-setting#<id>); the four that also carry a `persona` (jung / wallis / andreotti / bayo) can be conversed with on recursive.eco. Every voice obeys shared_intention — gate, not fate: a prompt, never a prophecy. The Golden Dawn is the esoteric register kept honest (pathworking, not prediction). NAME SCHOOLS, NOT LIVING PERSONS: voices drawn from living teachers are titled by their school and say 'inspired by' — DBT (←Linehan), Non-Dual Tantra (←Wallis), Post-Activism (←Akomolafe), Hospicing Modernity (←Andreotti). The `persona` field still carries the recursive.eco skill id (wallis / bayo / andreotti / jung), which can diverge from the voice `id`. Dead/iconic figures keep their own names (Kant, Jung). VOICE FIDELITY: every `how` sounds like the school/figure in its OWN vocabulary, not like us; `shared_intention.interpretation_note` is the user-facing disclaimer. The Ecosystem legitimately IS us — the project's own emergent-morality voice. PD/own content only; author PlayfulProcess.

shared_intention.title: Before you cast — the one intention
shared_intention.creed: Read to know yourself, not to be told your fate. A card starts a conversation with what is already alive in you — so hold what you find loosely, author its meaning yourself, and keep the choice your own. Be honest about what you see and kind in what you make it mean. Relate to the card; never obey it.
principles: Know, don't be told — a prompt, not a prophecy.
Hold it loosely — a meaning you can revise tomorrow.
Keep the choice yours — the card asks; you decide.
Be honest and kind — useful, true to your life, compassionate.
Stay humble — your reading is one true shape among others.
shared_intention.interpretation_note: A word of honesty: these voices are lenses, not people. DBT, Non-Dual Tantra, Post-Activism and Hospicing Modernity name schools of thought I was inspired by — drawn from the work of living teachers (Marsha Linehan, Christopher Wallis, Bayo Akomolafe, Vanessa Andreotti) who have never heard of this deck and endorse nothing here. Kant and Jung name their own traditions; the Ecosystem speaks for no one in particular — it is this project's own voice. Every reading is my interpretation.

voices.name: Kant
voices.how: Read from duty — the duty to know yourself — never to outsource your will. The card prompts; you legislate. Keep your autonomy, and refuse to be deceived, even by yourself.

voices.name: DBT
voices.how: Read from wise mind — reason and feeling together. Let the card hold two true things at once. Ask the three filters: is this reading useful, does it fit my life, is it kind? Relate to it; don't pass a verdict. (Dialectical Behavior Therapy — a lens inspired by Marsha Linehan.)

voices.name: The Ecosystem
voices.how: Read humbly. There is more than one healthy way to be. Tend, don't legislate — your reading is one wood among many, answerable only to whether it helps the whole of your life flourish.

voices.name: Jung
voices.how: Read as a mirror of the unconscious. The card you turn is the part of you not yet lived — the shadow, the archetype pressing to be seen. Don't explain it away; let it ask you what wants integrating.

voices.name: Non-Dual Tantra
voices.how: Read as līlā — the play of consciousness meeting itself. Hold the card with delight, not dread; lightly, not gravely. You are not being told anything; you are playing, and the play is the point. (Non-dual Śaiva Tantra — a lens inspired by the teaching of Christopher Wallis.)

voices.name: Post-Activism
voices.how: Slow down; the times are urgent. Let the card make a sanctuary for what is monstrous, stuck, or unspeakable. The crack is where something new gets in — resist the rush to resolve it. (Post-activism — a lens inspired by Bayo Akomolafe.)

voices.name: Hospicing Modernity
voices.how: Read to sit with complexity and your own complicity. Some of what the card shows is dying and wants hospicing; some is being born and wants midwifing. Stay with it; do not look away. (Hospicing Modernity — a lens inspired by Vanessa Andreotti and the GTDF collective.)

voices.name: The Golden Dawn
voices.how: Read as the Order did — pathworking. Each trump is a gate on the Tree of Life, a step between two sephiroth in the soul's ascent toward the light. Enter the card as an image and walk its path in contemplation; the Hebrew letter, the planet, the two spheres it joins are a map for the climb.
